altIdentifier.idno: Not in SC (late accession). Crostini Lappin, pp. 25–7

msDesc.head: Fragment of Gospel Lectionary for Saturdays and Sundays of the Octoechos; late 12th or early 13th century.
msDesc.msContents:
<<<<<<< Updated upstream
Gospel lectionary (fragment, one leaf only)
Gospel Book (fragment) =======
Gospel Lectionary (fragment) >>>>>>> Stashed changes
(fols. 1r–1v, l. 7) Pericope for the Third Sunday of the Octoechos (Mt. 6:24–33). Inc. mut.: ἀγαπή]σει · ἢ ἑνὸς ἀνθέξεται καὶ τοῦ ἑτέρου. Des.: ταῦτα πάντα προστεθήσεται ὑμῖν.
(fol. 1v, ll. 8–20) Pericope for the Fourth Saturday of the Octoechos (Mt. 8:14–17). Liturgical lemma: Σ(αββάτῳ) Δ̅ εὐ(αγγέλιον) κ(ατὰ) Μ(ατ)θ(αῖον) κε(φάλαιον) ξξ· λέ(γε) εἰς ἀσθενοῦντ(ας). Inc.: Τῷ καιρῷ ἐκείνῳ · εἰσῆλθεν ὁ Ἰ(ησοῦ)ς εἰς τὴν οἰκίαν πέτρου. Des.: καὶ τὰς νόσους ἐβάστασεν(·) +
(fol. 2r; verso of another leaf photographed here) Photograph of the verso of another leaf, formerly belonging to Prof. H. R. Willoughby (1890–1962). The leaf contains the pericope for Saturday (Mt. 9:18–26, from v. 24 in the photograph) and part of the pericope for Sunday of the Sixth week of the Octoechos (Mt. 9:1–6). Inc. mut.: ἀναχωρεῖτε · οὐ γὰρ ἀπέθανε τὸ κοράσιον ·. Liturgical lemma: Κυριακ(ῇ) στ̅ · εὐαγγέ(λιον) κ(ατὰ) Μ(ατ)θ(αῖον) κε(φάλαιον) θ̅ · ἦχ(ος) πλ(άγιος) α̅ εωθ(ινὸν) στ̅. Inc.: Τῷ καιρῷ ἐκείνῳ · ἐμβὰς ὁ Ἰ(ησοῦ)ς εἰς τὸ πλοῖον ·. Des. mut.: ἵνα δὲ εἰδῆτε ὅτι. Fol. 2v blank. A short note in Arabic is written in the left margin by the liturgical lemma, interpreted as “Sixth Sunday”. Clark describes the leaf as 25 × 19.4 cm, written surface 17 × 13.5 cm, one column, 19–21 lines per page, with initials in red and green, lection titles in uncials, and pencilled numeral “6” in the upper right of the recto. The measurements are close enough to those of the Bodleian leaf to make common provenance possible, though scribal identity is hard to prove.
Greek

extent.measure: Two front flyleaves, one parchment leaf plus one photograph, eleven back flyleaves

layoutDesc.layout: Ruled for 20/21 lines of text per page; very faint traces of a basic justification by a hard point are visible; the written space is c.160 × 130 mm; no prickings.

handDesc.handNote: Archaizing script of unsteady ductus and square modules, with square breathings; characteristic angular xi; zeta as a 3, often with prolonged, undulating upper and lower prongs; provincial characteristics such as theta “ad ombelico”; cursive nu in three angular segments.

physDesc.decoNote: Ornate four-line initial tau, drawn in black, carmine and green ink with volutes, and few extant touches of gold (fol. 1v: see illustration on page 24). The nuclei of several round letters are filled in green ink; it appears that some letters also bore touches of gold.

binding.p: Modern binding in parchment over pasteboards by Maltby, Oxford.

origin.note: A Melkite (Arabic-speaking Greek Orthodox) area is suggested by the style of script, decoration and the Arabic note on the margin of the other folio in the US.

provenance.p: The manuscript leaf bears notes in pencil on the lower left corner of fol. 1r, which may refer to a sale: “Greek XIII 3. 10. 0”, the ordinal indicating the century, the numeral, perhaps, a price. Number “3” is written in pencil on the top right corner of fol. 1r.
The manuscript leaf was given to the Bodleian Library by F. E. Norris in 1952, recorded in an ex-libris glued to the upper pastedown. Oval stamp of the Bodleian Library on right margin of fol. 1r.

The photograph was obtained by exchange from Prof. Harold Willoughby (1890-1962) in September 1954, as recorded in pencil on the lower margin of fol. 2r. Prof. Willoughby bought the leaf in 1935 from J. Tregaskis and Son, London: Caxton Head Catalogue 1013, no. 11. A cutting from the catalogue is glued to the upper pastedown, describing the leaf as containing “Marginal notes in Arabic”. It specifies a price of £3. Cf. S. De Ricciand W. J. Wilson, Census of Medieval and Renaissance Manuscripts in the United States and Canada, 3 vols., <<<<<<< Updated upstream New York, 1935–40, II, p. 2284 (addenda to p. 670).
The leaf formerly in the collection of Harold Willoghby, is now owned by ======= New York, 1935–40, II, p. 2284 (addenda to p. 670).

The leaf, formerly in the collection of Harold Willoghby, is now owned by >>>>>>> Stashed changes Bryan Blakemore of Springfield, Illinois, who obtained it form two former students—an elderly couple — who reported purchasing it at a local auction gallery in 2008 for around $20.

recordHist.source: B. Crostini Lappin, Catalogue of Greek Manuscripts Acquired...Since 1916, with additional information on the location of the Willoughby leaf.

ref.title: Pinakes | Πίνακες: Textes et manuscrits grecs

revisionDesc.change: James Cummings Up-converted the markup using https://github.com/jamescummings/Bodleian-msDesc-ODD/blob/master/convertTolkien2Bodley.xsl
